# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

# Final Secure Dockerfile for Python 2.7 Legacy Application

FROM python:2.7.18-slim-buster

# Create non-root user for running the application
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser -u 1001 appuser

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install security updates and necessary system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get upgrade -y && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        gcc \
        libc6-dev \
        libssl-dev \
        libffi-dev && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Upgrade pip and install security tools
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip==20.3.4 setuptools==44.1.1 wheel==0.37.1

# Copy requirements first for better layer caching
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser requirements.txt .

# Install Python dependencies with hash checking enabled
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --require-hashes --no-deps -r requirements.txt || \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application code with proper ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

# Remove write permissions from application files
RUN chmod -R 555 /app && \
    mkdir -p /app/logs /app/tmp && \
    chown -R appuser:appuser /app/logs /app/tmp && \
    chmod 755 /app/logs /app/tmp

# Switch to non-root user
USER appuser

# Set secure environment variables
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PYTHONHASHSEED=random \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1

# Expose application port (non-privileged)
EXPOSE 8080

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib; urllib.urlopen('http://localhost:8080/health').read()" || exit 1

# Run application as non-root user
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
